behavior_summary: This script contains a set of small utility routines that present in-game book text to the player. Each function spawns a Book::read process with a literal string (via str_to_ptr) and then yields/suspends until that reader process finishes.
No global flags are read or written; there is no branching or menu logic. Behavior is limited to launching a text reader and waiting for it to complete.

Each function corresponds to a different book/text (examples: "Honor Lost", "Expedition Journal, Book II", "De-venoming the Kith", poems and reports) and is likely invoked when the player interacts with or 'reads' the associated in-game book object.
notable_control_flow: Each internal function follows the pattern: spawn a Book::read process (process spawn operator '<=>') -> store returned handle in a temporary local -> execute 'suspend' to yield until Book::read completes.
There are no conditional branches, menus, or flag checks in these functions — purely spawn-and-wait behavior.
Functions appear to be utility/read handlers and do not call other internal functions in this file.
open_questions: How these functions are wired into the game's event system (which engine event or object registration calls U8P_STEVBOOK::func0080 and the helpers) is not present in the snippet and is inferred but not explicit.
Origin and scope of the variable 'pid' used in the spawn expression (pid <=> process ...) is not shown — is it a global runtime spawn helper or a compiler artifact?
Whether process_type 0x0001 implies these routines run as standard non-persistent utility functions or are registered as specific engine handlers is not fully specified in the excerpt.

main_function.name: U8P_STEVBOOK::func0080
main_function.purpose: Spawn a Book::read process to display the book text "Honor Lost" (literal) to the player and suspend until that process completes. Inferred to be the read/interaction handler for the associated in-game book.
entry_conditions: Likely triggered by a player interaction or 'read' action on the book object associated with this script.
Inferred: invoked when the engine requests presentation of the book's contents (player-initiated read/use).

function_map.name: Book::read

function_map.name: str_to_ptr
